add_on_services.name: Entryway Styling Package

add_on_services.name: Furniture Space Planning Add-On
add_on_services.description: Detailed furniture layout plan for one room, including ideal dimensions and circulation clearances.

add_on_services.name: Custom Sofa Specification
add_on_services.description: Specification sheet for a custom or semi-custom sofa tailored to your space and lifestyle.

add_on_services.name: Lighting Layering Plan
add_on_services.description: Ambient, task, and accent lighting strategy for one room, with fixture recommendations and placement.

add_on_services.name: Dimmer & Control Switch Plan
add_on_services.description: Recommendations for dimmers and smart switches to create flexible lighting scenes.

add_on_services.name: 3D Virtual Walkthrough Add-On
add_on_services.description: Interactive 3D walkthrough of one designed room using your approved design plan.

add_on_services.name: Mini Paint Color Consultation
add_on_services.description: Up to three coordinated paint color options with finish and sheen recommendations.

add_on_services.name: Materials & Finishes Review
add_on_services.description: Professional review of your selected finishes (tile, flooring, counters) with suggestions for improvements.

blog_articles.title: Small Apartment Layouts: 7 Smart Floor Plans Under 600 Sq Ft
blog_articles.excerpt: Make every square foot count with these real small apartment layout transformations and planning tips.
blog_articles.content:
Designing a small apartment is a puzzle of inches, not feet. In this guide, we break down seven real-world floor plans under 600 sq ft and show how we reconfigured circulation, storage, and furniture placement to unlock more usable space...

Section 1: Understanding circulation zones...
Section 2: Vertical storage and built-ins...
Section 3: Multifunctional furniture ideas...

Call-to-action: Ready for a tailored small apartment design plan? Our designers specialize in compact, efficient spaces.

blog_articles.title: Before & After: Small Apartment Makeover with Built-In Storage
blog_articles.excerpt: See how we turned a cluttered 450 sq ft rental into a calm, highly functional home using Scandinavian-inspired design.
blog_articles.content:
This 450 sq ft one-bedroom apartment suffered from oversized furniture and almost no built-in storage. The brief: create a calm, Scandinavian-inspired retreat with better flow and more concealed storage.

We started by mapping existing problem zones and identifying underused corners. Next, we introduced built-in wardrobes, a wall-to-wall media cabinet with hidden storage, and a fold-down dining table niche...

The result is a light, cohesive space with clear zones for relaxing, dining, and working.

If you’re ready to start your own small apartment design project, use the consultation form linked below to tell us about your space.

blog_articles.title: Scandinavian Living Rooms: Light, Cozy, and Functional
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how to bring Scandinavian warmth and simplicity into your living room with 5 key design moves.
blog_articles.content:
Scandinavian living rooms balance warmth and minimalism. In this article, we cover color palettes, materials, lighting, and styling principles you can apply right away...

Step 1: Start with a calm, light base palette.
Step 2: Layer natural textures.
Step 3: Prioritize functional, flexible lighting...

blog_articles.title: Designing a Home Office That Actually Works
blog_articles.excerpt: Ergonomics, focus, and storage: the three pillars of an effective home office design.
blog_articles.content: A home office needs to support your work habits as much as your personal style. We break down desk sizing, monitor placement, lighting strategies, and acoustics so you can work comfortably and efficiently...

blog_articles.title: How to Plan a Kitchen Renovation Budget
blog_articles.excerpt: Cabinets, counters, and contingencies: where your kitchen renovation dollars really go.
blog_articles.content: Kitchen renovations are one of the biggest investments you can make in your home. We unpack cost drivers, from cabinet construction to countertop materials and appliance packages, and show how to phase upgrades when you can’t do it all at once...

blog_articles.title: Case Study: Collaborative Office Tower with Biophilic Design
blog_articles.excerpt: A 160,000 sq ft office tower that combines flexible workspaces with lush indoor landscapes.
blog_articles.content: This office tower was conceived as a vertical campus, featuring flexible work neighborhoods, winter gardens, and a porous ground floor that blurs the line between public and private space...

blog_articles.title: Not Sure What Your Interior Style Is? Start Here.
blog_articles.excerpt: We created a quick style quiz to help you put a name to the look you love.
blog_articles.content: Clients often tell us they’re “a bit modern, a bit cozy, and definitely not formal”—but struggle to name their style. Our style quiz is designed to interpret your gut reactions to images and turn them into a clear direction for your project...

blog_articles.title: Turn Your Bedroom into a Sanctuary on Any Budget
blog_articles.excerpt: From quick decor shifts to full-service design, here’s how to upgrade your bedroom experience.
blog_articles.content: Bedrooms do more than host sleep—they’re where you decompress, read, and reset. We share three levels of bedroom upgrades, from simple styling tweaks to full-service design with custom built-ins...

blog_articles.title: Lighting Basics: Layering Light for Every Room
blog_articles.excerpt: The three types of lighting you need in every room and how to choose fixtures that work together.
blog_articles.content: Great lighting is more than a pretty pendant. We explain ambient, task, and accent lighting, and how to layer them for comfort and flexibility...

blog_articles.title: Sustainable Materials for Modern Interiors
blog_articles.excerpt: Low-VOC paints, FSC-certified wood, and other choices that make your home healthier and more sustainable.
blog_articles.content: If you’re renovating or furnishing, your material choices can dramatically affect indoor air quality and environmental impact. We outline key certifications and practical swaps you can make...

blog_articles.title: Rethinking the Open-Plan Office
blog_articles.excerpt: How to create focus, collaboration, and well-being in modern office layouts.
blog_articles.content: Open-plan offices aren’t going away, but they need to evolve. We share design moves that balance focus rooms, collaboration zones, and restorative spaces with plenty of natural light and greenery...

blog_articles.title: Inside a 280 Sq Ft Tiny House: Built-Ins That Do It All
blog_articles.excerpt: Tour a tiny home where every surface hides storage, seating, or both.
blog_articles.content: This tiny house project challenged us to think in inches. From a raised platform bed with deep drawers to a stair wall filled with cabinetry, we maximized storage without visual clutter...

portfolio_projects.title: Creative Hub Offices, Downtown District
portfolio_projects.short_description: A flexible office environment for a media agency with adaptable collaboration zones.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This 28,000 sq ft office spans two floors and was designed around the idea of a 'creative neighborhood' with focus rooms, team pods, and a central café stair. Exposed concrete and warm timber introduce an industrial yet inviting aesthetic, while acoustic treatments keep noise in check.

portfolio_projects.title: Lakeside Office Tower with Green Terraces
portfolio_projects.short_description: A high-rise office tower integrating outdoor terraces and biophilic interiors.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Conceived as a vertical campus, this 145,000 sq ft tower features multi-level green terraces that serve as breakout spaces and informal meeting areas. Floorplates are planned to maximize daylight penetration, while a highly efficient core frees up perimeter zones for flexible workstations.

portfolio_projects.title: Tech Campus Annex Offices
portfolio_projects.short_description: An adaptable annex building with hybrid work amenities and generous outdoor space.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Built as an expansion to an existing tech campus, this 52,000 sq ft annex includes modular project rooms, open collaboration zones, and a rooftop deck designed for events and all-hands meetings. The structural grid and MEP systems were planned to accommodate future reconfiguration.

portfolio_projects.title: Healthcare Provider Headquarters Fit-Out
portfolio_projects.short_description: Warm, patient-centered office interiors for a growing healthcare organization.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This 12,000 sq ft fit-out transformed a generic floor plate into a calming headquarters for a healthcare provider. Natural woods, soft palettes, and clear wayfinding ease stress for both staff and visitors.

portfolio_projects.title: Downtown Law Firm Office Renovation
portfolio_projects.short_description: A modern yet timeless office for a boutique law firm.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This 18,000 sq ft renovation balanced confidentiality with openness by introducing glass-fronted offices, acoustic partitions, and a central lounge that doubles as a casual meeting space.

portfolio_projects.title: Riverside Family Residence
portfolio_projects.short_description: A light-filled family home with indoor-outdoor connections.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This ground-up residence focuses on generous communal spaces, a central stair wrapped in oak, and expansive glazing that frames river views. Interiors pair warm wood with clean-lined furnishings.

portfolio_projects.title: Minimalist Downtown Apartment
portfolio_projects.short_description: A calm, minimalist apartment with concealed storage.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This 900 sq ft apartment renovation introduced concealed cabinetry, a built-in bench along the window wall, and a restrained material palette to create a sense of spaciousness.

portfolio_projects.title: Boutique Hotel with Local Craft Focus
portfolio_projects.short_description: A 90-key boutique hotel highlighting local craftsmanship.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Public areas feature custom lighting and locally produced textiles, while guest rooms are designed as quiet retreats. The lobby doubles as a daytime co-working lounge and nighttime bar.

portfolio_projects.title: Urban Retail Flagship Store
portfolio_projects.short_description: An immersive retail environment centered around materiality and light.
portfolio_projects.full_description: The flagship uses a restrained palette of concrete, oak, and glass to spotlight the merchandise. Flexible display fixtures allow for rapid reconfiguration of storytelling zones.

portfolio_projects.title: Mixed-Use Courtyard Block
portfolio_projects.short_description: A mixed-use block combining housing, retail, and flexible office with a shared courtyard.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Ground-floor retail supports the neighborhood, while upper floors contain a mix of apartments and flexible office spaces. A central courtyard brings daylight deep into the block and serves as a social heart.

portfolio_projects.title: Compact City Apartment with Sliding Partitions
portfolio_projects.short_description: A small apartment reconfigured with sliding partitions and built-ins.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This compact apartment uses sliding partitions to create privacy when needed while keeping the space open and bright the rest of the time. Built-in cabinetry hides clutter and integrates a fold-down desk.

portfolio_projects.title: Suburban Office Park Repositioning
portfolio_projects.short_description: Repositioning an aging office park into a contemporary workspace destination.
portfolio_projects.full_description: The project transformed dated lobbies, corridors, and landscapes into welcoming, amenity-rich environments. Upgrades include new entry canopies, shared conference centers, and outdoor work patios.

portfolio_projects.title: Flexible Co-working Space
portfolio_projects.short_description: A co-working interior with focus pods, hot desks, and event space.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Designed for a variety of workstyles, this co-working space features focus pods, open hot-desk zones, and a central cafe-bar that hosts events in the evenings.

professionals.name: Emma Larson
professionals.bio: Emma specializes in Scandinavian and minimalist interiors with a focus on natural light and simple, functional layouts.

professionals.name: Jacob Lee
professionals.bio: Jacob blends Scandinavian warmth with contemporary lines, creating bright, livable spaces for families and professionals.

professionals.name: Sophia Nyberg
professionals.bio: Swedish-born designer Sophia creates airy Scandinavian-inspired interiors with thoughtful storage and calm color palettes.

professionals.name: Marcus Hayes
professionals.bio: Marcus leads commercial office and mixed-use projects with an emphasis on flexible planning and sustainable systems.

professionals.name: Linda Cho
professionals.bio: Linda focuses on residential and small mixed-use projects with a clean modern aesthetic and thoughtful detailing.

professionals.name: Amy Rodriguez
professionals.bio: Amy specializes in warm modern interiors with layered textures and family-friendly materials.

professionals.name: Daniel Kim
professionals.bio: Daniel creates highly functional small apartments and condos with smart storage and multiuse furniture.

professionals.name: Hassan Ali
professionals.bio: Hassan designs contemporary office and institutional buildings with a focus on daylight and energy performance.

professionals.name: Nora Smith
professionals.bio: Nora’s interiors are eclectic yet edited, mixing vintage finds with clean-lined contemporary pieces.

professionals.name: Oliver Turner
professionals.bio: Oliver has led multiple mixed-use and hospitality projects with an emphasis on urban placemaking.

professionals.name: Claire Martin
professionals.bio: Claire designs calm, contemporary homes that prioritize comfort and natural materials.

professionals.name: Raj Patel
professionals.bio: Raj leads renovation projects for existing office and educational buildings, upgrading performance and usability.

professionals.name: Hannah Wilson
professionals.bio: Hannah focuses on bedrooms and living spaces that feel like retreats, using layered textiles and soft lighting.

professionals.name: Lucas Brown
professionals.bio: Lucas has a portfolio of boutique offices and retail projects with a strong industrial aesthetic.

professionals.name: Isabella Ramos
professionals.bio: Isabella brings a bohemian yet refined touch to interiors, layering patterns and color in a controlled way.

style_quiz_questions.text: When you imagine your ideal living room, which scene feels most like home?

options.text: Clean lines, neutral palette, and a few sculptural statement pieces.
Light wood, cozy textiles, and soft neutrals with a touch of greenery.
Leather sofa, exposed brick, and metal accents.
Patterned rugs, collected art, and a mix of old and new furniture.

style_quiz_questions.text: Which bedroom mood board would you choose?

options.text: Dark walls, rich woods, and brass accents for a dramatic feel.
Crisp white bedding, pale wood, and a simple upholstered headboard.
Mid-tone wood bed, graphic rug, and a few bold color pops.
Layered patterns, carved wood, and jewel-toned textiles.

style_quiz_questions.text: How do you feel about color in your main living spaces?

options.text: I love bold, saturated color on walls and big furniture pieces.
I prefer mostly neutrals with a few soft accent tones.
I like a balanced mix of neutrals and one strong accent color.
I gravitate toward classic, timeless color palettes.

style_quiz_questions.text: Which description best matches your ideal furniture shapes?

options.text: Curvy silhouettes and ornate details.
Simple, straight lines with rounded corners and little ornamentation.
Streamlined vintage pieces with tapered legs.
A mix of silhouettes from different eras.

style_quiz_questions.text: What kind of artwork and accessories do you love most?

options.text: Classic paintings, framed photography, and traditional decor pieces.
Minimal line drawings, abstract prints, and a few meaningful objects.
Bold graphic art and sculptural decor objects.
A layered gallery wall with collected pieces from travels.

style_quiz_questions.text: How formal or casual do you want your home to feel day-to-day?

options.text: Quite formal and polished, with everything in its place.
Relaxed, welcoming, and easy to keep tidy.
Casual and creative, with room to display collections.
Minimal and pared back, with only the essentials.

style_quiz_questions.text: Which materials are you most drawn to?

options.text: Dark woods, marble, and brass.
Light woods, cotton, linen, and wool.
Leather, steel, and exposed concrete.
Woven textures, carved wood, and patterned textiles.

style_quiz_questions.text: What’s most important to you when it comes to your home?

options.text: A sense of history and timelessness.
Calm, light, and an uncluttered feeling.
A bold, design-forward look that feels current.
A unique, collected space that tells my story.
